methods.name: Stage-Progression

methods.name: Peer-Median

methods.rationale: 23 same-sector peers exist in Space & Aerospace, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Open Cosmos is a Growth-stage Space & Aerospace company founded by Rafel Jorda Siquier, Jordi Barrera Ars, and Aleix Megias Homar. The company has raised $50M to date. The valuation universe includes 5 public Space & Aerospace comparables (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL) and 8 private peers, though direct peer matching at the Growth stage proved limited.

Of three valuation methods tested, only Stage-Progression was applicable. This method uses the $50M total raised as a base proxy and applies a 1.2x step-up multiplier typical for progression from Growth to the next institutional stage, yielding $60M. Peer-Median analysis was not viable due to insufficient narrow peer coverage—while 23 same-sector companies exist, only 1 adjacent-stage comparable met filter criteria, below the 3-datapoint minimum threshold. Public-Comp Anchoring could not be applied because the target's revenue was not available; the sector median P/S multiple of 53.2x across public comps cannot be deployed without this input.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $51M to $69M, with a median of $60M. This range reflects the single applicable method and carries material uncertainty. The primary constraint is lack of same-stage or same-tier private comparable transactions and absence of revenue data. Resolution of either constraint would materially strengthen conviction in the valuation midpoint.
